semanticCore: Confirming, strengthening, binding, making firm, or solemn swearing

keyDerivations.meaning: To confirm, strengthen, bind, make firm, or emphasize

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَا تَنْقُضُوا الْأَيْمَانَ بَعْدَ تَوْكِيدِهَا (9:91): And do not break oaths after their confirmation/strengthening.
discardedNoise: Akada (to confirm) - Form I variant (often exchanged in dialect).
summaryEn: The root represents binding or sealing an agreement firmly (Wakd), warning against breaking oaths that were sworn solemnly and confirmed in God's name.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر شد العقد وإحكامه وتأكيد العهد وتقويته والالتزام به (وكد)، ناهياً عن نقض الأيمان والعهود بعد توثيقها وتأكيدها باسم الله.
rawText: أَوْكَدَ 4 see 2. === أَوْكَدَتَاهُ يَدَاهُ His arms, or hands, exercised him with work. From a trad., relating to a seeker of knowledge. (L.) توكّد الأَمْرُ 5 and signify the same, [The thing, or affair, became confirmed, ratified, or corroborated]. (S, L, K. *) مَيَاكِيدُ and تَوَكِيدُ: see وِكَادٌ. مُتَوَكِّدٌ بِأَمْرٍ , (L,) or لِأَمْرٍ, (K,) Standing ready, or prepared, for a thing, or an affair (L, K.) مُوَاكِدَةٌ A she-camel that strives, or exerts herself, in her progress, course, or pace. (K.) وكّد 2 , (S, L,) inf. n. تَوْكِيدٌ; (S, K;) [and 🡻وَكَدَ as is implied in the K, but app. by the unintentional omission of the word وَكَّدَ;] and 🡻اوكد, inf. n. إِيكَادٌ; (S, L;) He made a horse's saddle, (S, L,) and a camel's, (L, K,) firm, fast, or strong; or bound or tied it firmly, fastly, or strongly; (S, L, K;) as also أَكَّدَ and آكَدَ: but more chaste with و. (S, L, K. *) === Also, He confirmed, ratified, or corroborated, a compact, a contract, a covenant, or an engagement; as also اكّد (S, L, K) and آكد: but, in this sense also, more chaste with و; (S, L;) or in this sense more approved with أ: (L:) and in like manner وكّد he confirmed an oath: you say, إِذَا عَقَدْتَ فَأَكِّدْ وإِذَا حَلَفْتَ فَوَكِّدْ When thou makest a contract, ratify; and when thou swearest, confirm. وَكَدَ 1 , aor. يَكِدُ, inf. n. وُكُودٌ, He remained, continued, stayed, abode, or dwelt, (L, K,) بِمَكَانٍ in a place. (L.) ___ See 2. ___ وَكَدَ أَمْرًا, (L, K, *) aor. يَكِدُ, inf. n. وَكْدٌ, (L,) He aimed at a thing; sought, endeavoured after, or pursued, it; desired it; intended it, or purposed it; syn. قَصَدَ (L, K) and طَلَبَ. (L.) See also below. === وَكَدَ وَكْدَهُ He directed his course to, or towards, him, or it; or he pursued his (another's) course; syn. قَصَدَ قَصْدَهُ; (S, L, K;) doing as he did. (L.) === وَكَدَ أَمْرًا, aor. and inf. n. as above, also signifies He laboured at, and endeavoured after, a thing. (L.) === وَكَدَ, (L, K,) aor. يَكِدُ, inf. n. وَكْدٌ, (L,) i. q. أَصَابَ He, or it, did, or went, right; attained an object; hit, struck, smote, affected, hurt, befell; &c.]. (L, K.) وَكْدٌ Desire; purpose; intention; aim; endeavour. (L, K.) See also 1. وُكْدٌ Work; labour; exertion; endeavour. (L, K.) Ex. مَا زَالَ ذٰلِكَ وُكْدِى That ceased not to be my work, (L, K,) and endeavour. (L.) وِكَادٌ A rope with which cows are tied on the occasion of milking. (S, L.) === Also وِكَادٌ and إِكَادٌ sings. of وَكَائِدُ [and أَكَائِدُ], (IDrd, L, K,) [pls. deviating from the constant course of speech in relation to the sings.; see art. أكد;] signifying, (i. e. the pls.,) Thongs, or straps, with which one binds (L, K) a camel's, or horse's saddle: (L:) or the thongs, or straps, by which the قَرَبُوس is bound to the two side-boards of a horse's saddle; (IDrd, L;) as also 🡻مَيَاكِيدُ and تَآكِيدُ and 🡻تَوَكِيدُ; (K;) or these are called مياكيد, but not تواكيد: (L:) and it [مياكيد] is a pl. that has no [proper] sing. (TA.)
